examples.title: BST Search Algorithm
examples.description: Recursive search for an element in Binary Search Tree
examples.code:
FUNCTION Search(root, key)
  IF root = NULL THEN
    RETURN NULL  // Element not found
  END IF
  
  IF key = root.data THEN
    RETURN root  // Found
  ELSE IF key < root.data THEN
    RETURN Search(root.left, key)  // Search left subtree
  ELSE
    RETURN Search(root.right, key)  // Search right subtree
  END IF
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Compares key with current node and recursively searches left or right subtree until found or NULL encountered. Follows BST property to eliminate half of remaining nodes each step.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) where h is height of tree

examples.title: BST Insertion in Python
examples.description: Insert a new node maintaining BST property
examples.explanation: Recursively traverses tree to find appropriate leaf position. If key is less than current node, goes left; otherwise goes right. Creates new node when NULL is reached.

examples.title: BST Deletion in C
examples.description: Delete a node from BST handling all three cases
examples.explanation: Three deletion cases: (1) Leaf - simply free and return NULL, (2) One child - replace with child, (3) Two children - replace with inorder successor and delete successor from right subtree.

examples.title: Inorder Traversal of BST
examples.explanation: Inorder traversal visits left-root-right producing sorted sequence. Finding min/max simply traverses to leftmost/rightmost node respectively.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for traversal, O(h) for min/max
